title: Activity Event – Status Picklist – Draft value available in Status field
description: AC 01.1 — Equivalence Partitioning — Draft is a valid picklist value in the Activity Event Status field.
preconditions:
- Admin user logged in to Salesforce
- Navigate to Activity Event object
steps_actions:
1. "Open Activity Event creation form"
2. "Click on the Status picklist field"
3. "Inspect the available picklist values"
steps_result:
1. "Activity Event creation form loads"
2. "Status picklist dropdown opens"
3. "'Draft' is listed as an available option alongside Published"

title: Activity Event – Create Form – Draft and Published selectable as status
description: AC 01.2 — Equivalence Partitioning — User can select either Draft or Published when creating a new Activity Event.
preconditions:
- Admin user logged in to Salesforce
- Navigate to Activity Event creation form
steps_actions:
1. "Open Activity Event creation form"
2. "Click on the Status picklist field"
3. "Select 'Draft' from the picklist"
4. "Re-open the Status picklist field"
5. "Select 'Published' from the picklist"
steps_result:
1. "Activity Event creation form loads"
2. "Status picklist dropdown opens with Draft and Published options"
3. "Status field displays 'Draft'"
4. "Status picklist dropdown opens again"
5. "Status field displays 'Published'"

title: Activity Event – Create Form – Default status is Published
description: AC 01.3 — Decision Table — When opening a new Activity Event creation form the Status field defaults to Published.
preconditions: - Admin user logged in to Salesforce
steps_actions:
1. "Navigate to Activity Event list page"
2. "Click 'New' to open the creation form"
3. "Observe the Status field value before any user interaction"
steps_result:
1. "Activity Event list page loads"
2. "Activity Event creation form opens"
3. "Status field shows 'Published' as the default value"

title: Activity Event – Draft – Mandatory field validation enforced on save
description: AC 02.1 — Negative Testing — Saving a Draft event with missing mandatory fields triggers validation errors.
preconditions:
- Admin user logged in to Salesforce
- Activity Event creation form open
- Status set to Draft
steps_actions:
1. "Set Status to 'Draft' on the creation form"
2. "Leave all mandatory fields empty"
3. "Click 'Save'"
4. "Observe the validation messages"
steps_result:
1. "Status field shows 'Draft'"
2. "Mandatory fields are blank"
3. "Save attempt is made"
4. "Validation error messages appear for each mandatory field (same as Published)"

title: Activity Event – Draft – All mandatory fields filled saves as Draft
description: AC 02.1 — Equivalence Partitioning — Saving a Draft event with all mandatory fields filled creates the event in Draft status.
preconditions:
- Admin user logged in to Salesforce
- Activity Event creation form open
steps_actions:
1. "Set Status to 'Draft'"
2. "Fill in all mandatory fields with valid data"
3. "Click 'Save'"
4. "Open the saved Activity Event record"
steps_result:
1. "Status field shows 'Draft'"
2. "All mandatory fields populated"
3. "Event is saved without errors"
4. "Activity Event detail page shows Status = Draft"

title: Activity Event – Draft – Add and remove participants
description: AC 02.2 — CRUD Testing — Participants can be added to and removed from a Draft Activity Event.
preconditions:
- Admin user logged in to Salesforce
- A Draft Activity Event already exists
steps_actions:
1. "Open the Draft Activity Event record"
2. "Navigate to the Participants related list"
3. "Add a participant to the event"
4. "Confirm the participant appears in the list"
5. "Remove the participant from the event"
6. "Confirm the participant is no longer in the list"
steps_result:
1. "Activity Event detail page opens with Status = Draft"
2. "Participants related list is displayed"
3. "Participant is added without errors"
4. "Participant name appears in the related list"
5. "Participant is removed without errors"
6. "Participant no longer appears in the related list"

title: Activity Event – Draft – Delete operation allowed
description: AC 02.3 — State Transition Testing — A Draft Activity Event can be deleted.
preconditions:
- Admin user logged in to Salesforce
- A Draft Activity Event already exists
steps_actions:
1. "Open the Draft Activity Event record"
2. "Click 'Delete' action"
3. "Confirm the deletion in the dialog"
4. "Search for the deleted event in the list view"
steps_result:
1. "Activity Event detail page opens with Status = Draft"
2. "Deletion confirmation dialog appears"
3. "Event is deleted"
4. "Event no longer appears in the list view"

title: Activity Event – Draft – Edit operation allowed
description: AC 02.4 — CRUD Testing — A Draft Activity Event can be edited and changes are saved.
preconditions:
- Admin user logged in to Salesforce
- A Draft Activity Event already exists with Event Name = 'Test Draft Event'
steps_actions:
1. "Open the Draft Activity Event record"
2. "Click 'Edit' to enter edit mode"
3. "Change the Event Name to 'Updated Draft Event'"
4. "Click 'Save'"
5. "Observe the updated record"
steps_result:
1. "Activity Event detail page opens with Status = Draft"
2. "Record enters edit mode"
3. "Event Name field updated"
4. "Changes saved without errors"
5. "Event Name shows 'Updated Draft Event' and Status remains Draft"

title: Activity Event – Draft – Duplication operation allowed
description: AC 02.5 — CRUD Testing — A Draft Activity Event can be duplicated.
preconditions:
- Admin user logged in to Salesforce
- A Draft Activity Event already exists
steps_actions:
1. "Open the Draft Activity Event record"
2. "Click 'Clone' or 'Duplicate' action"
3. "Observe the pre-filled creation form"
4. "Click 'Save' on the duplicated record"
5. "Open the newly created record"
steps_result:
1. "Activity Event detail page opens with Status = Draft"
2. "A new Activity Event creation form opens with pre-filled data"
3. "Fields are copied from the original Draft event"
4. "New event is saved without errors"
5. "A new Activity Event record exists with the copied data"

title: Activity Event – Status Transition – Draft to Published allowed
description: AC 03.1 — State Transition Testing — A Draft Activity Event can be transitioned to Published status via the status path bar.
preconditions:
- Admin user logged in to Salesforce
- A Draft Activity Event already exists
steps_actions:
1. "Open the Draft Activity Event record"
2. "Observe the status path bar (Draft → Published → Completed → Cancelled)"
3. "Click 'Published' in the status path bar"
4. "Click 'Mark as Current Event Status' confirm button"
5. "Observe the updated status"
steps_result:
1. "Activity Event detail page opens with Draft as active stage in path bar"
2. "Status path bar shows all 4 stages: Draft, Published, Completed, Cancelled"
3. "Published stage is selected in the path bar"
4. "Transition is confirmed"
5. "Published is now the active stage; success toast appears"

title: Activity Event – Status Transition – Draft to Completed blocked with EN error
description: AC 03.2 — Negative Testing — Attempting to transition a Draft event to Completed via the status path bar is blocked with an English error message.
preconditions:
- Admin user logged in to Salesforce (EN locale)
- A Draft Activity Event already exists
steps_actions:
1. "Open the Draft Activity Event record"
2. "Observe the status path bar (Draft → Published → Completed → Cancelled)"
3. "Click 'Completed' in the status path bar"
4. "Click 'Mark as Current Event Status' confirm button"
5. "Observe the error message"
steps_result:
1. "Activity Event detail page opens with Draft as active stage"
2. "All 4 stages visible in path bar including Completed"
3. "Completed stage is selected in the path bar"
4. "Transition is attempted"
5. "Error message displayed: 'You cannot mark a Draft event to Complete. Please Publish first. You cannot complete future event'"

title: Activity Event – Status Transition – Draft to Completed blocked with JP error
description: AC 03.2 — Negative Testing — Attempting to transition a Draft event to Completed via the status path bar is blocked with a Japanese error message. Requires SF user language set to Japanese.
preconditions:
- Admin user logged in to Salesforce
- SF user language set to Japanese (日本語) via Settings → Language & Time Zone
- A Draft Activity Event already exists
steps_actions:
1. "Change SF user language to Japanese: avatar → Settings → Language & Time Zone → Language = Japanese → Save"
2. "Open the Draft Activity Event record"
3. "Observe the status path bar in Japanese (下書き → 公開済 → 完了済 → キャンセル済)"
4. "Click '完了済' in the status path bar"
5. "Click '現在のイベントステータスとしてマーク' confirm button"
6. "Observe the error message"
steps_result:
1. "SF UI language switches to Japanese"
2. "Activity Event detail page opens with 下書き as active stage"
3. "Path bar shows all 4 stages in Japanese"
4. "完了済 stage is selected in the path bar"
5. "Transition is attempted"
6. "Error message displayed: '下書きのイベントを完了にすることはできません。イベントを公開してください。未来のイベントは完了できません'"

title: Activity Event – Status Transition – Draft to Cancel allowed
description: AC 03.3 — State Transition Testing — A Draft Activity Event can be transitioned to Cancelled status via the status path bar. After cancellation the edit form confirms Draft is no longer checked.
preconditions:
- Admin user logged in to Salesforce
- A Draft Activity Event already exists
steps_actions:
1. "Open the Draft Activity Event record"
2. "Observe the status path bar (Draft → Published → Completed → Cancelled)"
3. "Click 'Cancelled' in the status path bar"
4. "Click 'Mark as Current Event Status' confirm button"
5. "Observe the updated status in the path bar"
6. "Click 'Edit' to open the edit form"
7. "Observe the Activity Event Status radiogroup"
steps_result:
1. "Activity Event detail page opens with Draft as active stage in path bar"
2. "All 4 stages visible in path bar"
3. "Cancelled stage is selected in the path bar"
4. "Transition is confirmed"
5. "Cancelled is now the active stage; success toast appears"
6. "Edit form opens"
7. "Draft radio button is NOT checked in the Status radiogroup"

v2.id: 5. ""Activity Event detail page shows Status = Cancelled""

,,,2519,19,US03: Status Transitions,,,
,Activity Event – Status Transition – Published to Draft allowed (status path bar and edit form),AC 03.1 — State Transition Testing — A Published Activity Event can be reverted back to Draft status using either the status path bar or the Edit form Status radiogroup."
title:
- Admin user logged in to Salesforce
- An Activity Event exists in Published status (previously transitioned from Draft)
layer:
1. "Open a Published Activity Event record"
2. "Method 1 — Path bar: Click 'Draft' in the status path bar"
3. "Click 'Mark as Current Event Status' confirm button"
4. "Verify Draft is the active stage in the path bar"
5. "Transition event back to Published"
6. "Method 2 — Edit form: Click 'Edit' to open the edit form"
7. "Select 'Draft' in the Activity Event Status radiogroup"
8. "Click Save"
9. "Verify Draft is the active stage in the path bar"
steps_type:
1. "Activity Event detail page opens with Published as the active stage"
2. "Draft is visible in the path bar; clicking it selects it"
3. "Transition is confirmed"
4. "Draft is now the active stage in the path bar"
5. "Event returns to Published status"
6. "Edit form opens"
7. "Draft radio button is checked"
8. "Changes saved without errors"
9. "Draft is now the active stage in the path bar"

title: Activity Event – Draft – Not displayed in Learner app Calendar
description: AC 04.1 — Regression Analysis — A Draft Activity Event does not appear on the Learner app Calendar.
preconditions:
- A Draft Activity Event exists with a scheduled date
- A student is assigned as a participant to the Draft event
- Student credentials available for Learner app
steps_actions:
1. "Create a Draft Activity Event with a scheduled date and assign a student participant"
2. "Log in to the Learner app as the assigned student"
3. "Navigate to the Calendar view"
4. "Browse to the date of the Draft event"
5. "Search for the Draft event name"
steps_result:
1. "Draft Activity Event created with participant"
2. "Learner app dashboard loads"
3. "Calendar view is displayed"
4. "Calendar shows the scheduled date"
5. "The Draft event does NOT appear on the Calendar"

title: Activity Event – Draft – Not displayed in Learner app Booking system
description: AC 04.2 — Regression Analysis — A Draft Activity Event does not appear in the Learner app Booking system.
preconditions:
- A Draft Activity Event exists linked to an Event Master with booking enabled
- Student credentials available for Learner app
steps_actions:
1. "Create a Draft Activity Event linked to a bookable Event Master"
2. "Log in to the Learner app as a student"
3. "Navigate to the Booking section"
4. "Browse available events for the relevant date/location"
5. "Search for the Draft event name"
steps_result:
1. "Draft Activity Event created under a bookable Event Master"
2. "Learner app dashboard loads"
3. "Booking section is displayed"
4. "Available events are listed"
5. "The Draft event does NOT appear in the booking list"

title: Activity Event – Published – Displayed in Learner app Calendar after Draft to Published transition
description: AC 04.1 + AC 03.1 — Regression Analysis — After transitioning from Draft to Published the event appears in the Learner app Calendar.
preconditions:
- A Draft Activity Event exists with a scheduled date and a student participant
- Student credentials available for Learner app
steps_actions:
1. "Confirm the Draft event does NOT appear in Learner app Calendar"
2. "In Salesforce change the event Status from Draft to Published and save"
3. "Log in to the Learner app as the assigned student"
4. "Navigate to the Calendar view and browse to the event date"
steps_result:
1. "Draft event is hidden from Learner app"
2. "Event Status is now Published"
3. "Learner app dashboard loads"
4. "The event now appears on the Learner app Calendar"

title: Activity Event – Flag ON – Draft option available in Status picklist
description: AC 05.1 — Decision Table — When the feature flag is enabled the Draft option appears in the Activity Event Status picklist.
preconditions:
- Admin user logged in to Salesforce
- Feature flag for Draft Status is ON
steps_actions:
1. "Confirm the feature flag is enabled"
2. "Navigate to Activity Event creation form"
3. "Open the Status picklist dropdown"
4. "Inspect the available options"
steps_result:
1. "Feature flag is ON"
2. "Activity Event creation form loads"
3. "Status picklist dropdown opens"
4. "'Draft' is listed as an available option"

title: Activity Event – Flag OFF – Draft option hidden from Status picklist
description: AC 05.1 — Decision Table — When the feature flag is disabled the Draft option does not appear in the Activity Event Status picklist.
preconditions:
- Admin user logged in to Salesforce
- Feature flag for Draft Status is OFF
steps_actions:
1. "Confirm the feature flag is disabled"
2. "Navigate to Activity Event creation form"
3. "Open the Status picklist dropdown"
4. "Inspect the available options"
steps_result:
1. "Feature flag is OFF"
2. "Activity Event creation form loads"
3. "Status picklist dropdown opens"
4. "'Draft' is NOT listed as an available option"
